title: Phase 1C: add ferrotorch-core::signal::windows wrapping ferray-window
description:
Add a new ferrotorch-core::signal module wrapping ferray-window. Closes the torch.signal.windows gap flagged at the top level in docs/audit/README.md.

ferray-window provides:
- bartlett(m)
- blackman(m)
- hamming(m)
- hanning(m)
- kaiser(m, beta)

Scope:
- Add ferray-window = workspace dep in ferrotorch-core/Cargo.toml.
- Create ferrotorch-core/src/signal/mod.rs with a windows submodule.
- Each fn returns Tensor<f64> on Device::Cpu by default (windows are tiny precomputed coefficient vectors; no GPU compute needed). Caller can .to(Device::Cuda(_)) if they want it on device.
- Add: bartlett, blackman, hamming, hann (alias of hanning), kaiser, plus the typical extras users will want: cosine, exponential, gaussian, general_cosine, general_hamming, nuttall, parzen, taylor, tukey — these are NOT in ferray-window today. For Phase 1C scope, add only the 5 ferray-window provides; track the missing 9 as a follow-up issue. Pure-Rust window-fn implementations are short (~10 LOC each).
- Re-export at ferrotorch-core::lib.rs as  and .
- Tests: assert window length, symmetry, peak position.

GPU discipline:
- Windows are coefficient generators, not compute kernels. CPU output is correct and idiomatic. Document that the returned tensor lives on CPU and the user must move it to device.
- Do NOT add a fake GPU path that uploads a 2048-element vector via cudaMemcpy — that's worse than the CPU path.
- This is one of the few cases where /rust-gpu-discipline tolerates CPU-only because the math is trivial; document the choice in the module docstring.

torch.signal.windows is a top-level pytorch surface with no current ferrotorch home. This adds it.
